QF.id: VB Concept and Role of Mutual Fund_22

question.text: What is a "growth option" in a mutual fund scheme?

answer.text: Profits are reinvested in the fund, and the NAV increases over time — no regular payouts
The fund guarantees a fixed growth of 10% every year
Profits are paid out to investors every month as cash
Investors must add more money every year to the fund

explanation.text: **The Logic:** In the growth option, any profits earned by the fund (from dividends, interest, or capital gains) are reinvested back into the scheme. This means the NAV grows over time as profits accumulate. The investor does not receive any regular payouts — the growth shows up as a higher NAV when they eventually redeem. **The Trap:** Growth option does NOT mean guaranteed growth. It means profits are reinvested (not paid out). The actual growth depends on market performance. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is also tested in Series V-A (MF Distributors) under scheme options. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Growth option is suitable for clients who do not need regular income and want to build wealth over time. For retired clients needing monthly income, the IDCW (dividend) option or Systematic Withdrawal Plan (SWP) may be more suitable.
